title: Non-Invertible Symmetries on Tensor-Product Hilbert Spaces and Quantum Cellular Automata
authors: Rui Wen, Kansei Inamura, Sakura Schafer-Nameki
abstract: We investigate realizations of (1+1)-dimensional fusion category symmetries on tensor-product Hilbert spaces, allowing for mixing with quantum cellular automata (QCAs). It was argued recently that any such realizable symmetry must be weakly integral. We develop a systematic analysis of QCA-refined realizations of fusion categories and prove two statements. First, we show that, under certain physical assumptions on defects, any QCA-refined realization has QCA and symmetry-operator indices determined by the categorical data, up to the freedom of redefining the symmetry operators. Second, we construct a lattice model that provides a QCA-refined realization for any weakly integral fusion category symmetry on a tensor product Hilbert space. We also compute indices of the QCAs in our lattice model and show agreement with the first result. As an application of the general construction, we give an explicit QCA-refined realization of general Tambara-Yamagami categorical symmetries.
summary: The paper establishes that any weakly integral fusion category can be realized as a symmetry on a 1D tensor-product Hilbert space when allowing for Quantum Cellular Automata refinements. It provides a systematic way to calculate the indices of these symmetries and demonstrates the construction using anyon chains.
main_problem: Investigating the realizability of (1+1)-dimensional fusion category symmetries on tensor-product Hilbert spaces, specifically addressing how Quantum Cellular Automata (QCAs) refine these realizations.
main_result: The authors prove that any weakly integral fusion category admits a QCA-refined realization on a tensor-product Hilbert space and show that the indices of the symmetry operators and QCAs are uniquely determined by the categorical data.
method: The study uses fusion category theory, diagrammatic calculus, and the construction of anyon chain models to analyze the relationship between non-invertible symmetries and GNVW indices.
model_or_system: The paper focuses on (1+1)-dimensional quantum many-body systems, specifically 1D spin chains and anyon chains with tensor-product Hilbert spaces and finite-dimensional on-site degrees of freedom.
key_observables: Quantum dimensions, GNVW indices, left and right defect dimensions, and the index of Quantum Cellular Automata.
important_parameters: Weak integrality of the fusion category, grading group E, and the lattice quantum dimension.
main_assumptions: The proof relies on five physical assumptions regarding the locality of defects, the behavior of symmetry compositions, and the homogeneity of the index.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the rearrangement of local Hilbert spaces in anyon chains, the structure of the Ising category sector, and the sequential circuits used to map different Hilbert space sectors.
paper_structure: The paper begins by investigating constraints on QCA-refined realizations, moves to a general construction of lattice models for weakly integral categories, and concludes with specific applications to Tambara-Yamagami and Metaplectic categories.
why_interesting: This work provides a fundamental link between abstract algebraic structures (fusion categories) and concrete lattice models, which is essential for understanding the classification of non-invertible symmetries in many-body dynamics and topological phases.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: Rearranging the local Hilbert spaces for the anyon chain with a weakly-integral fusion category input. The red rectangle is a single site.
Figure 2: The trivial sector H1,f . At each link xi ∈{1, f }.
Figure 3: The nontrivial sector Hσ. The basis for each on-site Hilbert space is ti ∈{1, f }. The black dots are unique projections.
Figure 5: The circuit U2 σ. It is clear that U2 σ = T is translation by a lattice site to the left. The red rectangles show a site before and after translation.
